seoTitle: How to Set Up a Self-Employed Business in TurboTax
metaDescription: Learn how to set up your self-employed business profile in TurboTax, track expenses, and capture the deductions freelancers often miss.
intro: Setting up your self-employed business correctly in TurboTax is the foundation for capturing every deduction you're entitled to and staying compliant at tax time. This guide covers creating your profile, gathering the right forms, and tracking expenses accurately from the start. It's designed for freelancers, consultants, and small business owners, whether you're filing as self-employed for the first time or refining your setup from prior years.

steps.heading: Set up your TurboTax account for self-employment
steps.body: Start by selecting the self-employed filing option within TurboTax, which unlocks the forms and sections relevant to business income and expenses.

steps.heading: Create your self-employed profile
steps.body: Enter basic details about your business, such as the type of work you do, so TurboTax can tailor questions to your specific situation.

steps.heading: Review essential forms and documents
steps.body: Gather the forms you'll need, such as 1099s from clients, to ensure your reported income matches what was actually paid to you.

steps.heading: Track your business expenses accurately
steps.body: Enter your business-related expenses carefully, since accurately tracked costs directly reduce your taxable self-employment income.

steps.heading: Avoid common setup mistakes
steps.body: Watch for mixing personal and business expenses or missing income sources, both of which can lead to inaccurate returns or missed deductions.

faq.question: Do I need to set up a formal business entity to file as self-employed?
faq.answer: No, many freelancers file as self-employed sole proprietors without a formal business entity, and TurboTax's self-employed setup accommodates that.

faq.question: What counts as a deductible business expense?
faq.answer: Deductible expenses generally include costs directly related to running your business, such as supplies or software, though it's worth reviewing what qualifies for your specific situation.

faq.question: Do I need every client's 1099 before I file?
faq.answer: It's best to have all your 1099s and a record of total income before filing, since your reported income should reflect everything you earned, even without a form.

faq.question: Can I switch from a personal return to a self-employed setup mid-filing?
faq.answer: TurboTax generally allows you to add self-employment income and switch to the appropriate version as your filing situation requires it.

faq.question: Is self-employment setup harder for first-time freelancers?
faq.answer: It can feel more involved than a standard personal return, but TurboTax's guided questions are designed to walk beginners through it step by step.
